title: Houdini's Daring Dutch Escape
story: Handcuffs couldn't hold the great Harry Houdini! On January 21, 1903, the famous escape artist thrilled onlookers in Amsterdam. Houdini boldly challenged the Halvemaansteeg police station to lock him up. Within minutes, he slipped free from their toughest restraints, leaving officers scratching their heads. This daring feat cemented Houdini's reputation as the world's most incredible escape artist.

title: Rare Frilled Shark Caught on Camera
story: A mysterious creature from the deep sea made a surprise appearance in 2007. On January 21, the Awashima Marine Park in Japan captured incredible footage of a rare frilled shark. This ancient-looking fish, with its snake-like body and rows of needle-sharp teeth, is rarely seen by humans. Scientists were thrilled to study this living fossil, which has changed little in millions of years.
activity_question: What type of rare sea creature was filmed at the Awashima Marine Park?
bonus_fact: Frilled sharks can grow up to 6 feet long and live in deep ocean waters around the world.

title: Ancient Asteroid Shakes Up Earth's History
story: Scientists made a groundbreaking discovery in 2020! They found the world's oldest known asteroid impact site in Yarrabubba, Western Australia. This cosmic crash happened a mind-boggling 2.2 billion years ago. Researchers think this massive impact might have done more than just leave a crater - it could have helped end an ancient ice age on Earth. The findings were so important they were published in a scientific journal called Nature Communications on January 21.

title: Mars Rover Spirit Takes a Nap
story: Houston, we have a problem! On January 21, 2004, NASA's Mars Rover Spirit suddenly went silent. title: Neptune's Cosmic Dance with Pluto
story: Planets play a cosmic game of leapfrog! In 1979, Neptune jumped ahead to become our solar system's outermost planet. This celestial shuffle happened on January 21 when Pluto's unusual orbit brought it closer to the sun than its icy neighbor. Don't worry, though - this planetary dance is temporary. Pluto and Neptune regularly swap places in their long, elliptical orbits around the sun.

activity_answer: It moved closer to the sun

King's generous gift on January 21 ensured that future generations could explore and learn from his vast musical knowledge.
activity_question: What did B.B. King donate to the University of Mississippi?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to preserve music collections like this?

title: Rock Legends Honored in Hall of Fame
story: Music history was made in 1987 as the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame welcomed its second class of inductees. On January 21, a star-studded group of artists joined the ranks of rock royalty. Legends like Aretha Franklin, B.B. King, and Muddy Waters were celebrated for their groundbreaking contributions. The inductees represented a diverse range of styles, from blues to soul to early rock and roll pioneers.
activity_question: Which of these artists was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1987?
bonus_fact: The Rock and Roll Hall of Fame museum opened in Cleveland, Ohio, in 1995.

title: Gold Rush: Precious Metal's Price Soars
story: Glittering gold made history in 1980! On January 21, the price of this precious metal skyrocketed, briefly touching over $870 per ounce. By the end of the day, it settled at $850 an ounce - still an incredible record at the time. This golden moment sparked excitement in financial markets and had people everywhere talking about the value of this shiny treasure.
activity_question: What was the closing price of gold on this record-breaking day?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the price of gold can change so much in one day?

title: Opera's New Twist: Words Above the Stage
story: Imagine watching an opera and understanding every word! In 1983, the Canadian Opera Company made that dream come true. On January 21, they performed 'Elektra' with a groundbreaking addition: supertitles. These English translations appeared above the stage, helping the audience follow along. It was like having subtitles at the movies, but for live opera. Sonya Friedman created these first-ever opera supertitles, opening up the world of opera to more people than ever before.
activity_question: What did the Canadian Opera Company add to their performance of 'Elektra'?
follow_up_question: How do you think supertitles changed the opera-going experience for audiences?

title: A Powerful Voice for a President
story: Opera star Jessye Norman's voice soared through the air. She sang 'Simple Gifts' at President Ronald Reagan's second inauguration in 1985. Her performance on January 21 in Washington, D.C. captivated the crowd. Norman's rich, powerful voice added a touch of musical magic to this important political event.
activity_question: What song did Jessye Norman sing at the inauguration?
follow_up_question: Why do you think 'Simple Gifts' was chosen for this event?
